content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy claims "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" (https://runtracker.example/privacy), but RunSessionManager.swift:1–17 imports CoreLocation, requests when-in-use authorization, and stores GPS locations in `route`, matching the metadata claim "maps every run with GPS" (description.txt:1).
Pierre: Apple requires the privacy policy to accurately describe the app's actual data practices, and a direct contradiction on location is a classic 5.1.1 rejection trigger. The code plainly collects and stores location data on-device (and possibly iCloud via sync), so the "we do not collect location" statement is false even if data never leaves the device. Update the policy to disclose location collection, its purpose (run mapping), and where it is stored, and ensure the App Privacy label declares Location accordingly.
